_schema.description: Search results data for the e-shop prototype. Add a new key under `queries` for each test query you want to render with real data. Anything not listed falls back to `default` which renders placeholder (strike-through) content for usability testing.

shape.totalResults: number — shown next to 'Search results for ...'
shape.goods: array of { name, price, vatNote, stock: 'ok'|'low'|'none', badge, image }
shape.articles: array of { title, description, badges, action }

highlighted.title: Original Prusa MMU3 Lite (for CORE One)
highlighted.description: Multi-material upgrade for CORE One — print up to 5 colors or materials in one job. Open-frame variant, easy installation, and full PrusaSlicer integration.

goods.name: Original Prusa MMU3 Lite (for CORE One)
Original Prusa MMU3 Enclosed (for CORE One)
Original Prusa MMU3 Community Version Cable (for CORE One)
Original Prusa MMU3 (for MK4S/MK3.9S) — Full kit

articles.title: Original Prusa MK3.5 firmware 5.2.1 — MMU3 support
articles.description: Release notes for firmware 5.2.1 with full MMU3 support on MK3.5. New filament profiles, improved tip-shaping, and extended PrusaSlicer multi-material integration.

articles.title: MMU3 Compatibility
articles.description: Complete compatibility matrix: which Prusa printers support MMU3, which firmware versions are required, and how to migrate from MMU2S setups.

blog.title: Make your prints shine with colors! The MMU3 for the Prusa CORE One is Here!
blog.description: Multi-material printing comes to CORE One. Two MMU3 variants (Lite and Enclosed), seamless filament switching, and ready-to-print color profiles. Order now.

highlighted.title: Prusa USS Drybox
highlighted.description: Compact filament drybox for one spool. Built-in hygrometer, optimized airflow, and easy spool change. The reliable storage choice for hygroscopic materials like PETG, nylon and TPU.

articles.title: Prusa USS Drybox
articles.description: Hardware overview and setup guide for the USS Drybox. Covers humidity control, spool loading, and recommended storage conditions for hygroscopic filaments.

articles.title: Prusa Pro Filament Drybox
articles.description: Professional drybox documentation: hardware specs, dehumidifier operation, hygrometer calibration, and maintenance schedule for 24/7 production environments.

blog.title: Filament dryboxes and alternative spool holders — not only for MMU2S
blog.description: Why filament moisture matters, how to choose a drybox, and DIY alternatives. We cover hygrometer placement, desiccant types, and storage best practices for nylon, PETG and TPU.

highlighted.title: INDX 8-Tool Conversion Kit for CORE One/+
highlighted.description: Add 8-material printing to your Prusa CORE One/+. Includes 8 toolheads, dock, and full installation guide. Compatible with PrusaSlicer 2.7+.

goods.name: INDX 8-Tool Conversion Kit for CORE One/+
INDX 4-Tool Conversion Kit for CORE One/+

articles.title: Bondtech INDX — Add 8-Material Printing to Your Prusa CORE One+
articles.description: Step-by-step setup guide for INDX conversion kits. Covers unboxing, mechanical installation, firmware updates, and PrusaSlicer multi-material profiles.

blog.title: Prusa CORE One INDX — Unmatched Toolchanger With 8 Nozzles — Orders Now Open!
blog.description: Today we're opening orders for the INDX conversion kit. Eight independent toolheads, automatic tool docking, and seamless PrusaSlicer integration. Here's everything you need to know.

blog.title: Introducing the INDX! Fast and affordable 8-material printing exclusively on the CORE One
blog.description: A deep-dive into what makes INDX different from MMU3 and other multi-material systems. Build quality, speed comparisons, and material profiles.

highlighted.title: New Prusa CORE One+ 3D Printer
highlighted.description: The next evolution of our loved CoreXY printer — ready for INDX. Auto material switching, faster prints, quieter operation.

articles.title: CORE One — Getting Started Guide
articles.description: Unboxing, first calibration, and your first print. Covers cable routing, build plate prep, and PrusaSlicer profile selection.

articles.title: 6.4.0 (3.0.3) CORE One firmware release
articles.description: Improved Input Shaper auto-tuning, chamber temperature control, and new pause-mid-print behavior for filament changes.

articles.title: ABS, ASA and Nylon on CORE One — Chamber Temperature Guide
articles.description: CORE One heats chamber passively to 55 °C. Settings for engineering-grade materials and how to tune per filament brand.

articles.title: Calibration and maintenance schedule for CORE One
articles.description: Recommended monthly and quarterly checks: belt tension, idler condition, bearing lubrication, and Z-axis re-leveling.

blog.title: Exploring CORE One+: New Features, Upgrades, INDX
blog.description: A deep-dive into what makes the CORE One+ different from CORE One. AMS-style material switching, chamber control, and the upcoming INDX dock.

blog.title: Behind the design of CORE One — interview with the engineering team
blog.description: Why CoreXY, why now, and what we learned from MK4. The team behind the printer talks frame stiffness, belt routing and acoustics.

blog.title: Top 10 community prints made on CORE One in March
blog.description: A roundup of the most impressive things makers printed on CORE One last month — drone frames, articulated dragons, lithophanes.

blog.title: From MK4 to CORE One — migration guide for power users
blog.description: Bring your PrusaSlicer profiles, settings, and bed sheets across. Differences in build volume, speed, and accuracy benchmarks.
